features.summary: Johnson & Johnson reported strong Q4 2021 results with worldwide sales of $24.8 billion (10.4% growth) and adjusted EPS of $2.13 (14.5% growth). Full year 2021 sales reached $93.8 billion with 13.6% growth. The company delivered its tenth consecutive year of above-market pharmaceutical growth, continued medical device recovery despite COVID-19 impacts, and competitive consumer health performance amid supply constraints. J&J announced plans to separate its Consumer Health business in 2023, creating two independent companies.
features.management_outlook: Management provided 2022 guidance of 7.0-8.5% operational sales growth ($100.3-101.8 billion) and adjusted EPS of $10.60-10.80. The company expects COVID-19 vaccine contributions of approximately $3 billion in sales and $0.20 in EPS. New CEO Joaquin Duato outlined three priorities: making Medical Devices a best-in-class performer, delivering pharmaceutical commitments toward $60 billion by 2025, and successfully executing the Consumer Health separation. The company maintains confidence in long-term growth potential despite near-term headwinds from inflation and COVID-19 variants.
features.risks: Key risks include ongoing COVID-19 impacts on medical device procedures, particularly from the Omicron variant affecting hospital staffing and elective surgeries. Supply chain constraints continue to impact Consumer Health, especially in Skin Health and Beauty products. Inflationary pressures across raw materials, labor, and transportation are affecting margins. The upcoming STELARA patent expiration in September 2023 presents biosimilar competition risk. Hospital staffing shortages and reduced office visits reported by insurers could impact procedure volumes and pharmaceutical demand.
features.key_drivers: Growth drivers include strong pharmaceutical portfolio performance with DARZALEX (+33.4%), TREMFYA (+82.8%), and ERLEADA (+61.3%) leading oncology and immunology segments. Medical Devices showed improving competitiveness with market share gains across most platforms and over 20 new product launches in 2021. The company's strong financial position with nearly $32 billion in cash enables aggressive M&A strategy and continued R&D investment, which reached a record $14.7 billion in 2021. Digital surgery initiatives including MONARCH robotic system and VELYS platform expansion support future growth.
